subject: Reasoning & Mental Ability
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match kiya waha answer bhara hai (answer_source dekho).

questions.question_text: If cost of 15 eggs is ₹75, then find out the cost of 4 dozens eggs.
A group of 1200 persons consisting of captains and soldiers is travelling in a train. For every 15 soldiers, there is one captain. The number of captains in the group is:
Find the least number by which 175760 be divided to make it a perfect cube.
What is the smallest number of ducks that could swim in this formation - two ducks in front of a duck, two ducks behind a duck, and a duck between two ducks?

Geetika's mother remembers that Geetika's exam is after 21st, but before 26th of March, her sister remembers that her exam is after 22nd, but before 25th of March and her father remembers that her exam is on odd date. On which date in March is Geetika's exam?

Study the given information carefully and answer the following question.
Swati, Chetna and Rajni like to play badminton.
Chetna and Rajni like to play hockey.
Swati and Chetna like to play ludo.
Swati likes to play hockey and cricket.
Who among the following likes to play all the four games?
Kirti has 8 dolls and 7 hair clips. How many different combinations of 1 doll and 1 hair clip can she make?
Pointing towards a boy, Sonia says, "The boy is the son of the only child of my father." How is Sonia related to that boy?
If Puneet went to park everyday in March 20XX except the dates which are multiples of 3, then for how many days did he go to the park?
If Mohit is shorter than Sahil but taller than Rohit. Rishi is the tallest. Anuj is shorter than Sahil but taller than Mohit. Who is the shortest?

Kaushal celebrates his daughter's birthday on two days after the second Thursday of September 20XX.
On which day will he celebrate his daughter's birthday?

There are five friends and each has a pet. The pets are dog, cat, rat, fish and parrot. A and B do not like cats and dogs. B and C do not like fishes and parrots. C and D do not like cats and rats. D and E do not like fishes. Who has the dog?
How many different possible combinations of 1 cap and 1 T-shirt can be formed from the given caps and T-shirts?
Introducing a man, Kanika said, "He is the only son of my mother's mother". How is Kanika related to the man?

There are five friends and each has a pet. The pets are dog, cat, rat, fish, and parrot. A and B do not like cats and dogs. B and C do not like fishes and parrots. C and D do not like cats and rats. D and E do not like fish. Who has the fish?

There are five friends and each has a pet. The pets are dog, cat, rat, fish and parrot. A and B do not like cats and dogs. B and C do not like fishes and parrots. C and D do not like cats and rats. D and E do not like fish. Who has the cat?

There are five friends and each has a pet. The pets are dog, cat, rat, fish, and parrot. A and B do not like cats and dogs. B and C do not like fishes and parrots. C and D do not like cats and rats. D and E do not like fish. Who has the peacock?

There are five friends and each has a pet. The pets are dog, cat, rat, fish and parrot. A and B do not like cats and dogs. B and C do not like fishes and parrots. C and D do not like cats and rats. D and E do not like fish. Who has the rat?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting to the left of S?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting to the right of P?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting to the right of R?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting to the left of Q?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting between P and S?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting between R and P?
A two-digit number which is greater than 20, less than 35 and has nine in one's place.
Five boys took part in a race. Raj finished before Mohit but behind Gaurav. Ashish finished before Sanchit but behind Mohit. Who won the race?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting between T and R?
Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Who is sitting between Q and S?
A two-digit number which is greater than 55 and less than 61, and is a multiple of 5.
A two-digit number which is a multiple of 4, 6 and 8 and is less than 30.

Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Now if A and B join the table such that A sits between P and T, whereas B sits between Q and R. Who is sitting to the left of A?

Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Now if A and B join the table such that A sits between P and T, whereas B sits between Q and R. Who is sitting to the right of B?
A two-digit number which is greater than 86, less than 100 and has 4 in one's place.

Five friends are sitting around a round table in a restaurant. P is sitting next to Q. R is sitting between Q and S and T is sitting to the left of P. Now if A and B join the table such that A sits between P and T, whereas B sits between Q and R. Who is sitting to the left of P?
Varun, Raj and Mohit are good in Science. Mohit and Raj are good in Maths and English, Varun and Raj are good in Computer. Sachin is good only in English. Who is good in all subjects?
A two-digit number which is greater than 30, less than 35 and multiple of 4.

Five students from section A, B, C, D and E each are selected for a debate competition. Tom and Jack are not in section B, C or D. David and Jim are not in A or B. Tom and David are not in section C or E. The fifth friend is Betty. Who is in section A?

Five students from section A, B, C, D and E each are selected for a debate competition. Tom and Jack are not in section B, C or D. David and Jim are not in A or B. Tom and David are not in section C or E. The fifth friend is Betty. Who is in section C?
An even two-digit number which is greater than 96.

Five students from section A, B, C, D and E each are selected for a debate competition. Tom and Jack are not in section B, C or D. David and Jim are not in A or B. Tom and David are not in section C or E. The fifth friend is Betty. Who is in section E?
A two-digit number which is a multiple of 9 and 11.

Five students from section A, B, C, D and E each are selected for a debate competition. Tom and Jack are not in section B, C or D. David and Jim are not in A or B. Tom and David are not in section C or E. The fifth friend is Betty. Who is in section B?
If you write down all the numbers from 1 to 100, then how many times do you write 3?
A two-digit number which is greater than 90 and has 7 in ones' place.

Five students from section A, B, C, D and E each are selected for a debate competition. Tom and Jack are not in section B, C or D. David and Jim are not in A or B. Tom and David are not in section C or E. The fifth friend is Betty. Who is in section D?
A family of three people has 3 cars. The father and the son do not drive the black car. The mother and the son do not drive the white car. Who drives the white car?
A two-digit number which is divisible by 7 and has 0 in ones' place.
A family of three people has 3 cars. The father and the son do not drive the black car. The mother and the son do not drive the white car. Who drives the red car?
A two-digit number which is greater than 37, has 3 in tens' place and is divisible by 3.
A family of three people has 3 cars. The father and the son do not drive the black car. The mother and the son do not drive the white car. Who drives the black car?

Ravi and Kunal are good in Hockey and Volleyball. Sachin and Ravi are good in Hockey and Baseball. Gaurav and Kunal are good in Cricket and Volleyball. Sachin, Gaurav and Michael are good in Football and Baseball.
Who is good in Hockey, Cricket and Volleyball?
A two-digit number which is divisible by 5, have 6 in tens' place and is not a multiple of 10.

Ravi and Kunal are good in Hockey and Volleyball. Sachin and Ravi are good in Hockey and Baseball. Gaurav and Kunal are good in Cricket and Volleyball. Sachin, Gaurav and Michael are good in Football and Baseball.
Who is good in Baseball, Cricket, Volleyball and Football?
The sum of two numbers is 6 and the difference is 0. Guess the two numbers.
The sum of the two numbers is 18 and the difference is 0. Guess the two numbers.
The sum of the two numbers is 14 and the difference is 2. Guess the two numbers.
The sum of two numbers is 24 and the difference is 8. Guess the two numbers.

Read the information given below and answer the question that follows:
There is a group of five girls.
Garima is second in height.
Priya is taller than Latika and shorter than Garima.
Manha is the tallest.
Megha is shorter than Latika.
Who is the shortest in height?
The difference of two numbers is 6 and their sum is 8. Guess the two numbers.

Read the information given below and answer the question that follows:
There is a group of five girls.
Garima is second in height.
Priya is taller than Latika and shorter than Garima.
Manha is the tallest.
Megha is shorter than Latika.
Who is shorter than Manha but taller than Priya?
The difference of two numbers is 1 and their sum is 11. Guess the two numbers.

Study the following information carefully and answer the question based on it.
There are four friends who opt for subjects according to their interests.
Only Rahul and Shailesh opt for Maths.
Vaibhav dislikes only Maths and Chemistry.
Shalabh likes Physics, Chemistry and Biology.
Rahul dislikes only Physics and Shailesh dislikes Physics and Biology.
Who likes Physics but not Chemistry?
The difference of two numbers is 3 and their sum is 15. Guess the two numbers.

Study the following information carefully and answer the question based on it.
There are four friends who opt for subjects according to their interests.
Only Rahul and Shailesh opt for Maths.
Vaibhav dislikes only Maths and Chemistry.
Shalabh likes Physics, Chemistry and Biology.
Rahul dislikes only Physics and Shailesh dislikes Physics and Biology.
How many like Chemistry?
The sum of two numbers is 14 and the difference is 6. Guess the two numbers.
The sum of two numbers is 25 and the difference is 15. Guess the two numbers.
The difference of two numbers is 30 and their sum is 90. Guess the two numbers.

Some names of students and types of pens are given below. How many different combinations of 1 kind of pen and 1 student can be formed?



Name of Student:


Vinay


Sneha


Tarun


Ajay


Priya


Mini

Study the following information carefully.
Trishika, Sidak and Sanchi likes ice-cream.
Sidak and Sanchi likes burger.
Trishika and Sidak likes pizza.
OnlyTrishika likes burger and chowmein.
Who among the following likes all the four food items?

Read the given information carefully and answer the following question.
Karan and Kartik like Maths but do not like Science.
Kartik and Shikha do not like Hindi but like English.
Shikha and Sonal like Maths and Science.
Karan likes only one subject.
Sonal does not like only English.
Which of the following subjects is liked by all the four students?
A, P, R, X, S and Z are sitting in a row. S and Z are in the centre and A and P are at the ends. R is sitting on the left of A. Then who is sitting on the right of P?
Sanchi has 6 tops and 4 pair of jeans. How many different combinations of 1 top and 1 pair of jeans can she wear?
If Kirti goes for Karate classes on even number of days only in March 20XX, then for how many days does she go for Karate classes in that month? (Assume that all Sundays are holidays.)

Raghav remembers that his sister's birthday is after 12th, but before 15th of October, while his father remembers that his daughter's birthday is after 13th, but before 17th of October. On which date in October, is Raghav's sister's birthday?
How many different pairs of 1 girl and 1 boy each can possibly be formed from 4 girls and 3 boys?

Vishal starts his guitar classes from 1st Wednesday of July 20XX. If he takes leaves on odd number of days, then for how many days does he go for guitar classes in that month? (Assume that every Sunday is a holiday.)
